mnemonics: TEST ML: Train, Evaluate, Split, Test - Model Learning
Don't TEST on TRAINed data (TRAIN/TEST SPLIT)
keyPoints: A core principle is to test the model on unseen data (a holdout set) to evaluate its generalization ability.
Common evaluation metrics include Accuracy, Precision, Recall, F1-Score, and ROC-AUC, chosen based on the problem type.
Techniques like k-Fold Cross-Validation provide a more robust estimate of model performance by repeatedly training and testing on different data subsets.
commonMistakes: Testing on the training data, which leads to overfitting and an overly optimistic performance estimate.
Using the test set to make model improvement decisions (like tweaking hyperparameters), which effectively 'contaminates' the test set and makes it a second validation set.
Choosing an evaluation metric without considering the class imbalance or the specific business goal (e.g., prioritizing Precision over Recall for fraud detection).
